steps.title: Find the slope of s
steps.content:
Rewrite line $s$ in slope-intercept form:
$$
10x+2y=0
$$
$$
2y=-10x
$$
$$
y=-5x
$$
So line $s$ has slope $-5$.

steps.title: Use perpendicular slope
steps.content: Line $t$ is perpendicular to line $s$, so its slope is the negative reciprocal of $-5$, which is $\frac{1}{5}$.

steps.title: Use the two points
steps.content: The slope between $(7,0)$ and $(k,\frac{6}{5})$ is $\frac{1}{5}$: $$ \frac{\frac{6}{5}-0}{k-7}=\frac{1}{5} $$ $$ 6=k-7 $$ $$ k=13 $$ The answer is 13.
